name: aidrift
description: Watches your AI coding chats (Claude Code, Codex) and warns when sessions drift.

commands.title: Drift: Sign In with Email + Password
Drift: Create Checkpoint for Active Session
Drift: Show Status for Active Session
Drift: Open Active Session in Dashboard

viewsWelcome.contents:
Welcome to AI Drift — watch your AI coding chats for drift.

[Sign in to AI Drift](command:aidrift.loginWithBrowser)

Opens drift.geniohub.com in your browser. After signing in, the token is sent back to this editor automatically.

[Other sign-in options](command:aidrift.login)

[Switch profile](command:aidrift.switchProfile) to sign into a different host (e.g. local dev).

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with AI Drift
walkthroughs.description: Sign in and start tracking your AI coding sessions.

steps.title: Sign in to AI Drift
steps.description:
Click the button below to open the AI Drift dashboard in your browser. After you sign in or sign up, the token is delivered back to this editor automatically — no copy-pasting.

[Sign in via browser](command:aidrift.loginWithBrowser)

steps.title: Switch between hosts (dev vs prod)
steps.description:
AI Drift supports multiple named profiles. The default profile points at https://drift.geniohub.com. Add a local dev profile or switch between them from the status bar or the command palette.

[Switch profile](command:aidrift.switchProfile)
[Add profile](command:aidrift.addProfile)

steps.title: You're set
steps.description:
AI Drift now watches Claude Code and Codex transcripts in your workspace. Open the AI Drift view in the activity bar to see live sessions and scores.

[Show AI Drift view](command:workbench.view.extension.aidrift)

aidrift.apiBaseUrl.description: DEPRECATED — use 'Drift: Switch Profile' instead. This value is ignored once profiles are configured (the active profile's API URL wins).

aidrift.dashboardUrl.description: DEPRECATED — use profiles instead. The active profile's dashboard URL is used.

aidrift.watchClaudeCode.description: Watch ~/.claude/projects/ for new Claude Code transcripts and auto-track them.

aidrift.watchCodex.description: Watch ~/.codex/sessions/ for new OpenAI Codex rollouts and auto-track them.

aidrift.trackTaskExecution.description: Report VSCode task and terminal shell outcomes (lint/build/test pass/fail) for the active turn so the Code Quality dashboard has data. Terminal coverage relies on VSCode shell integration (1.93+).

aidrift.watchGitEvents.description: Watch .git/refs/ for commits and pushes. Displays git events in the session timeline and analytics dashboards.

aidrift.statusPollIntervalSeconds.description: How often the status bar polls /sessions/:id/status. On repeated failures the interval grows exponentially (capped at 5 minutes) and resets on first success.

aidrift.requestTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for HTTP requests from the extension to the AI Drift API. Requests that exceed this are aborted so a hung network doesn't wedge polling. Minimum 2 seconds.

aidrift.driftAlertThreshold.description: Score below which a drift alert fires.

aidrift.autoCheckpoint.description: Auto-create a checkpoint on every accepted turn.

aidrift.implicitAcceptTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds without a follow-up before a turn is treated as implicitly accepted.

aidrift.sessionsLimit.description: Maximum number of recent sessions to fetch for the AI Drift sidebar. Sessions are filtered to the current workspace after fetching, so the visible count may be lower. Use the sidebar's 🔍 Search Sessions command to find older sessions beyond this limit.
